---
name: Philosophical discussion
about: Deep questions about design principles, the Cave/Republic architecture, credentials, or civic purpose
title: '[Philosophy] '
labels: philosophy
assignees: ''
---

## The question

State it plainly. What design decision, principle, or tension are you raising?

## Background

Why does this matter? What are you observing in the tool, the codebase, or the world that brought you to this question?

## The tension

Most philosophical questions in The Republic come from a genuine tension between values. For example:
- Legibility vs. dependency (the Illich test)
- Anonymity vs. accountability (credentials and pseudonymity)
- Accessibility vs. capture-resistance (ease of participation vs. credential gates)
- Speed vs. accuracy (AI hallucination risk vs. slower verified approaches)

Name the tension you're working with.

## Your position

Where do you land? This is not required — it's fine to open a question you don't have an answer to — but if you have a view, state it.

## Prior art

Has this question been answered elsewhere? In civic tech, in political philosophy, in adjacent disciplines? Reference the relevant work if you know it.

## What would change

If this question were resolved in your direction, what would be different about the tool? Be specific — it helps ground abstract discussion in concrete implications.

---

*Philosophical discussion issues are not a path to bypassing design decisions through attrition. A question that has already been addressed in PHILOSOPHY.md may be closed with a pointer to the relevant section. Good-faith engagement with the existing reasoning is expected before proposing to change it.*
